title: Fix /init hydration: project name precedence, goal auto-fill, conventions over-inference

problem.summary: The /init Phase 2.5 hydration script has three review findings that deviate from the original PRD intent.

findings.title: Project name precedence is wrong
findings.current_behavior: project_name fallback chain is README heading, package.json name, git remote name, directory name.
findings.expected_behavior: project_name fallback chain is git remote name, package.json name, README heading, directory name.
findings.because: The git remote is the canonical repository identifier, while README headings are prose and may not match project identity.

findings.title: Goal field should not be auto-filled from README
findings.current_behavior: project_desc is auto-filled from the first README paragraph and written into the project goal slot.
findings.expected_behavior: project_desc remains <!-- TODO: fill in your project goal -->.
findings.because: The project goal is a human-authored slot; README first paragraphs often contain badges, install text, or marketing prose.

findings.title: Conventions hydration over-infers for Frontend and Scripts rows
findings.current_behavior: Frontend and Scripts language/notes are inferred aggressively from the detected backend language.
findings.expected_behavior: Frontend is populated only with frontend framework evidence; Scripts infers TypeScript only from tsx or ts-node and otherwise defaults conservatively.
findings.because: Conventions require evidence. False precision is worse than a human-facing TODO marker.

in_scope: .omp/commands/init.md Phase 2.5 Python block: fix project name precedence, goal auto-fill, and conventions over-inference.
out_of_scope: Other init.md phases: Phase 1, Phase 2, Phase 3, and Phase 4.
br/bv commands, workflow gate, and extensions.

key_files.notes: Phase 2.5 Python block; relevant regions around lines 169-176 and 307-320.
Authoritative PRD source mirrored by this JSON file.

systems_touched: Memory file template substitution for project.md and conventions.md.
existing_code_not_to_modify: Backend, tech-stack, gotchas, and decisions hydration logic except where directly coupled to the listed fixes.

approach.title: Project name precedence
approach.change: Reorder the project_name fallback chain to git_remote_name(), package_json.name, first_heading_from_readme(), root.name, then "Untitled Project".
approach.rationale: A simple order change fixes the behavior without adding functions or state.

approach.title: Goal/description auto-fill
approach.change: Set project_desc directly to <!-- TODO: fill in your project goal --> and remove README paragraph auto-fill from that slot.
approach.rationale: The project goal is intentionally human-authored.

approach.title: Conservative conventions hydration
approach.change: Detect frontend framework dependencies before populating Frontend; infer TypeScript scripts only from tsx or ts-node; otherwise keep conservative placeholders/defaults.
approach.rationale: Evidence-based hydration prevents false conventions from being written into durable project memory.

outcome: Project name derivation uses git-remote-first precedence.
The goal/description field is a human-only TODO marker and is never auto-filled.
Conventions Frontend/Scripts rows are populated only when concrete evidence exists; otherwise they remain placeholder-marked for human review.

requirements.text: In init.md Phase 2.5 Python block, project_name fallback chain is git_remote_name() → package_json.name → first_heading_from_readme() → root.name → "Untitled Project".
requirements.acceptance: Inspect .omp/commands/init.md Phase 2.5 Python block and confirm project_name uses git_remote_name() before package_json.name, README heading, root.name, and "Untitled Project".

requirements.text: In init.md Phase 2.5 Python block, project_desc always resolves to <!-- TODO: fill in your project goal --> with no README auto-fill.
requirements.acceptance: Inspect .omp/commands/init.md Phase 2.5 Python block and confirm project_desc is the TODO marker and does not read from README prose.

requirements.text: In init.md Phase 2.5 Python block, Frontend row only hydrates when frontend framework deps are detected in package.json: react, svelte, vue, next, nuxt, astro, angular, or solid.
requirements.acceptance: Inspect .omp/commands/init.md Phase 2.5 Python block and confirm Frontend language/notes are populated only from detected frontend framework dependencies.

requirements.text: In init.md Phase 2.5 Python block, Scripts language infers TypeScript only when tsx or ts-node is a dependency; otherwise it defaults to Bash.
requirements.acceptance: Inspect .omp/commands/init.md Phase 2.5 Python block and confirm Scripts uses TypeScript only with tsx or ts-node and Bash otherwise.

requirements.text: Conventions rows for Frontend/Scripts leave <!-- TODO: fill in --> when evidence is absent.
requirements.acceptance: Run or inspect the hydration logic for a repo without frontend framework or script-runner evidence and confirm Frontend/Scripts retain TODO placeholders where evidence is absent.

requirements.text: Existing correct hydration for Backend, tech-stack, gotchas, and decisions is unchanged.
requirements.acceptance: Compare the changed .omp/commands/init.md Phase 2.5 Python block against the prior behavior and confirm Backend, tech-stack, gotchas, and decisions hydration logic is not changed except as required by the listed findings.

requirements.text: The script remains idempotent: re-running /init on an already-hydrated repo does not overwrite user edits.
requirements.acceptance: Run /init or the Phase 2.5 hydration path on already-populated memory files and confirm user-populated fields are not overwritten.

constraints: Changes are scoped to the Python embedded block in .omp/commands/init.md, Phase 2.5.
No changes to the init command structure, other phases, or memory file templates.
Must preserve idempotency: only replace exact template placeholders, never overwrite user-populated fields.

risks.impact: Changing project name precedence could change the heading on already-initialized repos if /init is re-run.
risks.mitigation: The existing already-populated guard means previously hydrated files are left alone.

risks.impact: Conservative conventions hydration means more TODO markers for humans to fill.
risks.mitigation: This is intentional; false precision is worse than a TODO marker.

success_criteria: Project name derivation uses git-remote-first precedence.
The goal/description field remains a human-only TODO marker and is never auto-filled from README prose.
Conventions Frontend/Scripts rows are populated only when concrete evidence exists; otherwise they remain placeholder-marked for human review.
